channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Xbox Unveils New Improvements to Achievements
item.content:encoded:
Xbox is changing how Achievements work, introducing new visuals, usability improvements, and more. Insiders can test it starting today.

It’s been some time since we saw a new feature being tested by the Xbox Insiders. Last month, we saw a select group of players try out features like groups on home, custom colors, disable Quick Resume, and more. Now, the latest introduction brings a massive overhaul to how Xbox Achievements look and work.

Xbox Insiders Can Test the New Achievements Starting Today

A new Xbox Wire post details all the improvements made to Achievements. First and foremost, the look and feel have been changed with updated icons and animations for rare and classic achievements. These Notifications will also match the players’ chosen custom color, introduced in the previous Insider update.

There’s a new feature that allows players to hide any game from their Achievement history. Players should note that this only affects the profile’s appearance; hidden games will still count towards the player’s total Gamerscore.

Another new feature on Xbox Achievements is to highlight the games that you have 100% completed. Players will also find new filter options in this view to better manage their achievements.

The new Achievements improvements are available to select Xbox Insiders starting today, so they can begin testing the updates immediately. More Insiders will receive the new update over time, with “broader availability coming later to all players.”

In other news, the price and release date details of the limited edition Forza Horizon 6 Xbox Accessories have been revealed. Additionally, the line-up for the first wave of titles to arrive on Xbox Game Pass during April has been revealed, and it’s full of heavy hitters. What are your thoughts on the new improvements to Xbox Achievements? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Forza Horizon 6’s limited edition Xbox wireless controller and headset. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: PlayStation State of Play May Happen Next Week, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
NateTheHate provides a new update on when players can expect the next Sony PlayStation State of Play.

The PlayStation State of Play presentations are Sony’s equivalent to Nintendo Directs, and where PS5 owners can expect the latest updates on games coming to PlayStation and games being made by the in-house studios. Now, reliable leaker NateTheHate has revealed that the next Sony presentation is tentatively scheduled for Thursday, April 16. However, that date is unconfirmed

Sony PlayStation Games Set for 2026

At the most recent State of Play, players received news on Marvel Tōkon: Fighting Souls, the return of Castlevania, and even a shadow-dropped God of War experience in God of War: Sons of Sparta. Looking ahead, the Marvel fighting game is slated for release this August, SAROS is coming out shortly, and the upcoming Wolverine game from Insomniac is slated for September.

Additionally, there could be news on the upcoming 007 First Light game as well as other cross-platform games like LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight. Speaking of the 007 game, it was recently announced that a limited-edition PlayStation 5 DualSense controller will be released alongside the game. Learn more about that here.

NateTheHate’s Recent Leaks

While Nate is a reliable leaker, it is important to reiterate that the April 16 date is not yet confirmed. Lately, players have been looking to Nate for news on Nintendo’s offerings for this year.

He recently released a podcast in which he shared all the news he had heard about the major Nintendo releases for the year. Of note, Nate believes the next 3D Mario title will not be released until 2027. However, a major Nintendo staple is slated to return.

Nate says that Star Fox will finally have a new release this summer. Also, that game is expected to be revealed later this month. Learn more about the forthcoming reveal at this link.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor this story and report any updates on a potential Sony PlayStation State of Play. We will also provide full coverage of the event whenever it takes place.

When do you think the next Sony State of Play will be? What are you hoping to see at the presentation? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1755: Hints and Answer for April 9, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1755 for April 9, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, April 9, 2026

Today’s Wordle answer for game #1755 is a tough one. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 4.3 out of 6 guesses. That makes it slightly easier than the 4.8-guess average from April 8.

General Wordle Hints For April 9

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: It describes something heavily loaded or weighed down
  • Hint 2: Often used for ships, animals, or people carrying a lot
  • Hint 3: Suggests a sense of burden or fullness

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1755 for April 9, 2026.

Starter Words For April 9, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • DANCE
  • LANCE
  • LANED

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1755 On April 9, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: LADEN

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

LADEN is an adjective that means “burdened; loaded down.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1754 on April 8, 2026

Answer: INLET

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1755?

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads up. The game on April 10 will be Wordle Game #1756.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: PGL Bucharest 2026 Heads to Playoffs: Who are the Contenders?
item.content:encoded:
PGL Bucharest 2026 is moving into its playoff stage on Thursday, following the conclusion of a Swiss stage that saw Astralis and FUT continue their growth, but also saw the continued struggles of BC.Game and FaZe.

Teams such as Parivision, 3DMAX, The MongolZ, and Astralis will all be eyeing up the $400,000 first place prize money in a rare opportunity without Counter-Strike’s top dogs present.

PGL Bucharest prepares for playoffs with Astralis and FUT flying high

FUT and Astralis are undoubtedly the hot hands at the event after 3-0 finishes in the groups. Victories over Parivision and The MongolZ in the 2-0 matchups were reflective of two growing rosters.

After years of struggle, Astralis are increasingly growing a strong base for contention, with Jakob ‘jabbi’ Nygaard averaging a 1.40 HLTV rating so far, giving Astralis some star power.

FUT were at their explosive best as they outgunned Parivision in their qualification match, showing the roster’s innate potential, which is now being realized in Tier 1 competition.

Elsewhere, 3DMAX recovered from missing out on the Cologne Major with a 3-1 group record, equalling The MongolZ and MIBR’s run. After years away from Tier 1 CS, Kévin ‘misutaaa’ Rabier actually showed a great level in Bucharest as the team looked to rebuild.

The MongolZ seem amongst the contenders at the event despite the prolonged absence of their coach.

The roster’s synergy with Anarbileg ‘cobrazera’ Uuganbayar seems to be growing day by day, but their one-dimensionality can bite them without Erdenedalai ‘maaRaa’ Bayanbat there to add a touch of depth.

Despite their status as favourites before the event, Parivision barely snuck into the playoffs with a 3-2 record, playing out an incredibly close series against Wildcard, requiring quintuple overtime on Mirage. Overall, their level will have to sharpen up for the playoffs.

Eyeballers were the surprise package of the final Swiss Stage matches, defeating FOKUS to put Jesper ‘JW’ Wecksell back in a big-event playoffs, with B8 also joining them with a 3-2 record that encapsulates their inconsistency.

The PGL Bucharest playoff bracket is:

  • MIBR vs 3DMAX
  • Astralis vs Eyeballers
  • FUT vs B8
  • The MongolZ vs Parivision

No signs of progress for the vanquished BC.Game and FaZe

With the PGL Bucharest invites stretching so low into the ranking, the lowest-ranked teams didn’t have much expectation on their heads. That was not true for BC.Game and FaZe.

For BC.Game there were no excuses. The roster had to perform here after their recent results saw them miss the Major and effectively leave the Tier 1 invite bubble. Instead, it was more of the same disconnected CS with farcical performances at times.

They will now look to IEM Atlanta in May as a shot to gain some VRS, but it seems unlikely. Frankly, the BC.Game experiment seems totally doomed to fail, to the surprise of absolutely no one.

FaZe’s collapse has been much less expected after making the StarLadder Budapest Major Grand Final. The roster effectively started the event at 0-2 after forfeiting to attend HLC Pro Belgrade in their last effort to make the Cologne Major.

That failed; they also failed in their one match at this event after a truly awful loss to Inner Circle. The rebuild will already be under way in the FaZe organization. It is over.

Who will take home the PGL Bucharest trophy? Discuss that on our Discord server!
For more CS news, see our IEM Rio 2026 tournament guide.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
